title: Write docs/deploy.md: Docker-based ops runbook
description:
Tie together the password-reset script, migration-strategy, and Docker bundle tasks into a single ops runbook, so deploying/operating/recovering the app doesn't rely on tribal knowledge. Extends (doesn't duplicate) the README's existing one-paragraph '## Production' section.

## Implementation -- cover:
- Env var reference: DTASKS_INVITE_CODE, DTASKS_SECURE_COOKIES, DTASKS_SESSION_DIR, DTASKS_DB_PATH, and where they're set (compose env file).
- Build & deploy steps: docker compose build && docker compose up -d.
- Migration step: how/when 'alembic upgrade head' runs in the entrypoint.
- Backup/restore instructions (cron + sqlite .backup against the volume).
- How to run scripts/reset_password.py against the running container.
- Invite-code handout process for beta friends.
- One-line note for future-you: this VM may host additional unrelated apps later, each as a separate container + separate Caddy site block -- no action needed now.
acceptance_criteria: A person with no prior context can follow docs/deploy.md alone to: deploy from scratch, recover a lost password, and restore from backup.

title: Dockerize dtasks + Caddy for single-VM deployment (Oracle Always Free/PAYG)
description:
Package the app for the chosen hosting target: a single Oracle Cloud 'Always Free' compute instance, upgraded to Pay-As-You-Go billing (removes idle-instance reclamation risk; usage stays $0 as long as it remains within Always Free resource caps -- 2 OCPU/12GB RAM, 200GB storage as of the June 2026 allocation). PAYG upgrade was requested and is pending as of 2026-07-13; note it triggers a one-time card verification hold (commonly reported around $93-100, refunded -- sometimes with a multi-day delay), which is separate from ongoing usage billing.

Docker was chosen over bare systemd specifically because this VM may host additional unrelated hobby apps later, each isolated in its own container.

## Implementation
- Dockerfile: multi-stage build -- stage 1 builds the frontend (npm run build -> frontend/dist), stage 2 is the Python runtime that serves it via the existing create_static_files_router setup in main.py; runs the migration-then-uvicorn entrypoint from the migration-strategy task.
- docker-compose.yml: app service + Caddy service, sharing a network; named volume for the SQLite DB file + FileStore session directory so data survives container recreation/image updates.
- Caddyfile: domain name -> automatic HTTPS via Let's Encrypt -> reverse_proxy to the app service.
- Env var wiring for prod: DTASKS_INVITE_CODE, DTASKS_SECURE_COOKIES=1, DTASKS_SESSION_DIR, DTASKS_DB_PATH pointed at the mounted volume paths, passed via a compose env file (not committed to git).
- Backup strategy: cron on the host running sqlite3 .backup against the volume-mounted DB path (or via docker compose exec) -- cron + sqlite .backup, not litestream (overkill for a friends beta).
- Oracle/VM prerequisites (ops steps, not app code): confirm PAYG upgrade completed; install Docker + the docker compose plugin on the VM.

## Verification
- Manual smoke checklist: friend signs up over HTTPS with the invite code; Set-Cookie shows Secure + HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax; anonymous /api/* returns 401.
- Restarting the containers (docker compose down && up) does not lose existing data (DB + sessions survive).
acceptance_criteria: A friend can sign up with the invite code over HTTPS on the deployed instance; session cookie has Secure+HttpOnly flags in prod; container restarts preserve DB and session data; achieved via docker compose up on the Oracle VM.

title: Decide migration strategy: alembic upgrade head vs create_all
description:
Alembic is already fully scaffolded (backend/migrations/, 3 revisions: 0001 baseline, 0002 multitenancy, 0003 green tasks) but backend/app/main.py's lifespan still unconditionally calls Base.metadata.create_all -- the two mechanisms currently coexist undocumented. This is a real risk for a fresh prod DB: it's unclear which one is authoritative, and a bug here silently produces schema drift relative to migration history.

## Implementation
- Make alembic authoritative for prod: remove/gate create_all() so it is not called at prod app startup (keep it for test fixtures only -- backend/tests/conftest.py).
- Docker entrypoint (coordinate with the Docker bundle task): container startup runs 'alembic upgrade head' before starting uvicorn, so a fresh container always has an up-to-date schema with no manual step.
- Document the decision inline only where non-obvious; the full narrative goes in docs/deploy.md.

## Verification
- Fresh empty DB + 'alembic upgrade head' produces the same schema as today's create_all().
- Existing test suite stays green (tests keep using create_all() directly, unaffected by the prod code path change).
acceptance_criteria: main.py no longer calls create_all() at prod startup; a fresh container running the entrypoint ends up with a fully migrated schema; test suite passes unchanged.

title: Password reset script for beta users
description:
Manual account-recovery path for the multitenancy beta (no email/SMTP). Part of dtask-y23 (Production deployment setup), epic dtask-tc7.

## Implementation
- scripts/reset_password.py <email>: prompts interactively for a new password (never accept it as a bare CLI arg -- avoids shell-history/process-list leakage); reuses backend/app/auth.py's hash_password.
- Connects directly to the DB via the DTASKS_DB_PATH env var (same one the app uses); looks up the user by lowercased/stripped email; updates password_hash.
- Must work invoked against the Docker deployment, e.g. docker compose exec app python scripts/reset_password.py <email>, since the DB lives on a volume mounted into the container. Invocation gets documented in docs/deploy.md.

## Verification
- Test: after running the script logic against a test DB, login with the old password fails and login with the new one succeeds.

## Note
A prior attempt at this script existed (orphaned .pyc bytecode files were found in scripts/__pycache__ and backend/tests/__pycache__) but the .py source was never committed -- rebuilding from scratch.
acceptance_criteria: Running the script against a real user updates their password hash such that the old password no longer authenticates and the new one does; script prompts for the password interactively rather than accepting it as a CLI argument.

title: Add default task projects for Work and Personal
description: Create predefined default projects for Personal and Work sections, auto-assign tasks to them if no project is specified, and prompt user if ambiguous.

title: Production deployment setup (VPS, HTTPS, ops scripts)
description:
Phase 4 of multitenancy epic dtask-tc7. Depends on dtask-3l0 + dtask-13p (done). Data migration/backfill was descoped: the owner will re-enter their data manually in a fresh account, so no create_owner backfill is needed. The old pre-multitenancy rows (user_id IS NULL) in the dev DB are invisible to all accounts and can simply be deleted.

## Implementation
1. scripts/reset_password.py <email>: prompts new password, updates the hash (reuse auth.hash_password). This is the manual recovery path — beta has no email/SMTP.
2. Prod config: DTASKS_INVITE_CODE set; Secure cookie flag on via DTASKS_SECURE_COOKIES=1; session FileStore dir (DTASKS_SESSION_DIR) on persistent disk; DTASKS_DB_PATH pointing at a persistent SQLite file. Caddy in front for automatic HTTPS, reverse-proxying to uvicorn on localhost. systemd unit (or docker-compose) for the app process.
3. Fresh prod DB: just run `alembic upgrade head` (no stamp needed on a brand-new DB); or rely on create_all at startup — pick one and document it.
4. Ops notes in docs/deploy.md (or README section): env vars, build steps (npm run build so the backend serves frontend/dist), backup strategy (cron sqlite .backup or litestream), how to hand out the invite code.

## Verification
1. reset_password test: after running the script logic, login with the old password fails, with the new one succeeds.
2. Manual smoke checklist on the VPS: friend signs up over HTTPS with the invite code; Set-Cookie shows Secure + HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax; anonymous /api/* returns 401.
acceptance_criteria: A friend can sign up with the invite code over HTTPS on the deployed instance; session cookie has Secure+HttpOnly flags in prod; password reset script works; deploy steps are documented.

title: Frontend: landing page, signup/login, route guard, logout
description:
Phase 3 of multitenancy epic dtask-tc7. Depends on dtask-xwy (auth endpoints). Public landing page, auth flows, route guard, logout. No cookie plumbing needed — app is same-origin in dev (Vite proxy /api -> :8000) and prod (dist served by backend).

## Implementation
1. api/types.ts: User {id, email}. api/client.ts: authMe(), signup({email, password, invite_code}), login({email, password}), logout().
2. Auth state in api/hooks.ts: useCurrentUser() = useQuery({queryKey: ['auth','me'], queryFn: api.authMe, retry: false, staleTime: Infinity}). Mutations: useSignup / useLogin (on success invalidate ['auth','me'] and navigate '/'), useLogout (POST logout, qc.clear(), navigate '/welcome').
3. Router restructure (router.tsx): rootRoute currently renders Layout directly. Make root pathless with a plain Outlet; add (a) /welcome public route and (b) a pathless authed layout route (component Layout) wrapping all existing child routes. Authed layout beforeLoad: await queryClient.ensureQueryData for auth/me; on 401 throw redirect({to: '/welcome'}). /welcome beforeLoad: if already authed throw redirect({to: '/'}).
4. Global 401 handling: construct QueryClient with QueryCache/MutationCache onError — if ApiError.status === 401 and current route isn't /welcome, redirect to /welcome (covers session expiry mid-use). Extract as a testable handleAuthError(err) helper; do NOT handle 401 per-hook.
5. views/WelcomeView.tsx: short app pitch + form toggling between signup (email, password, invite code) and login (email, password); submit via useSignup/useLogin; render ApiError detail (403 bad invite, 409 duplicate email, 401 bad credentials); disable submit while pending.
6. Sidebar: show current user's email + Logout button (useCurrentUser + useLogout). Existing Sidebar.test.tsx will need the auth query mocked.

## TDD plan — vitest + testing-library, fetch mocked via vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch') per existing convention in api/hooks.test.tsx
1. hooks.test: useSignup POSTs /api/auth/signup with {email, password, invite_code}; invalidates ['auth','me'] on success.
2. hooks.test: useLogin same for /api/auth/login; on error surfaces ApiError and leaves cache untouched.
3. hooks.test: useLogout POSTs /api/auth/logout and clears the query cache.
4. WelcomeView.test: renders signup fields; submits entered values; shows server error detail on 403/409/401; toggles to the login form.
5. handleAuthError unit test: 401 -> redirect target /welcome; other errors ignored.
6. Router guard integration test (createMemoryHistory + real route tree): me -> 401 renders WelcomeView for '/'; me -> 200 renders Layout; /welcome while authed redirects to '/'.
7. Playwright e2e (repo already has Playwright): visit / -> redirected to /welcome -> sign up with invite code -> starter data visible -> logout -> login again -> data still there. This is the end-to-end acceptance gate for the whole epic UI.
acceptance_criteria: Anonymous visitor landing on any route is redirected to /welcome; can sign up with invite code and lands in the app with starter data visible; can log out and log back in; expired/invalid session mid-use redirects to /welcome instead of showing broken views; frontend tests cover guard and signup/login forms.
close_reason: Implemented and verified all tests passing in parallel worktrees

title: Backend tenancy: scope all queries per user + isolation tests
description:
Phase 2 of multitenancy epic dtask-tc7. Depends on dtask-xwy — the user_id columns and the authed/multi-client test fixtures land there. This phase is pure application logic: scope every query to the authenticated user. Invariant to enforce: no handler ever queries by bare primary key.

## Implementation
1. DI: provide user: User to handlers (dependency reading request.user set by SessionAuth); add it to every existing handler signature in main.py.
2. Helpers gain a user_id parameter baked into the WHERE clause:
   - _get_project(session, project_id, user_id): add Project.user_id == user_id.
   - _get_task(session, task_id, user_id): select(Task).join(Project).where(Task.id == task_id, Project.user_id == user_id).
   - set_habit_log's habit lookup: add Habit.user_id == user_id.
   - _active_must_have_count(session, user_id, exclude_task_id): join(Project) + Project.user_id == user_id. Without this, other users' must-haves consume a user's daily limit of 2 (existing global-count bug).
3. List/create scoping:
   - list_projects / list_habits: .where(<Model>.user_id == user.id).
   - create_project: stamp user_id=user.id AND compute next position only over the user's own projects (select(Project.position).where(Project.user_id == user.id)) — otherwise positions interleave across users.
   - create_task: parent fetched via scoped _get_project, inherits safety.
   - reorder_task: task fetched scoped; the follow-up project fetch passes user.id too.
4. Cross-tenant semantics: scoped helpers fall through to NotFoundException -> 404, byte-identical to a nonexistent id. Never 403 — resource existence must not leak.

## TDD plan — write tests/test_isolation.py first (two clients via make_client fixture from phase 1; users A and B each get starter data at signup)
1. test_lists_are_scoped — A and B each see exactly their own 4 starter projects / 4 habits; id sets are disjoint.
2. test_cross_tenant_project_404 — B GET/PATCH/DELETE on A's project id -> 404 with the same body as a nonexistent id (e.g. 999999).
3. test_cross_tenant_task_404 — B PATCH/DELETE/reorder A's task; B POST /api/projects/{A's project}/tasks -> all 404; verify A's data unchanged afterwards.
4. test_cross_tenant_habit_404 — B PUT /api/habits/{A's habit}/log -> 404.
5. test_must_have_limit_is_per_user — starter data ships with 2 active must-haves per user, i.e. the limit starts maxed: A unsetting one of theirs then setting another succeeds even while B sits at the limit; A at the limit still gets 409 independent of B's state.
6. test_project_positions_are_per_user — A and B each create one project; both get position 4, not 4 and 5 (regression for the cross-user position interleave).
Regression gate: test_projects.py and test_auth.py stay green.
acceptance_criteria: All existing endpoints operate only on the authenticated user's data; isolation test suite passes: B gets 404 on all of A's resource IDs across every endpoint (GET/PATCH/DELETE/reorder/log), B's lists contain only B's rows, must-have limit is per-user.
close_reason: Implemented and verified all tests passing in parallel worktrees

title: Backend auth: User model, sessions, auth endpoints
description:
Phase 1 of multitenancy epic dtask-tc7. Blocks dtask-3l0 (tenancy scoping) and dtask-13p (frontend). ALL schema changes for multitenancy land here in one Alembic revision (users table + nullable user_id on projects/habits) so phase 2 is pure application logic.

## Dependencies to add (backend/pyproject.toml)
- argon2-cffi (Argon2id password hashing)
- alembic (first migration story; schema currently comes from Base.metadata.create_all)

## Implementation
1. models.py: User(id, email String(255) unique — store lowercased/stripped, password_hash String(255), created_at). Add to Project and Habit: user_id FK users.id ondelete=CASCADE, index=True, nullable at DB level until phase 4 backfills prod (application always sets it).
2. Alembic: init with async template (alembic init -t async), sqlalchemy.url from main.DB_PATH, render_as_batch=True in env.py (SQLite ALTER TABLE limitations). Two revisions: 0001 baseline = current pre-multitenancy schema (so the existing prod DB can be stamped in phase 4), 0002 = users table + user_id columns. Tests keep using Base.metadata.create_all (models stay source of truth); Alembic is for real DBs.
3. New module app/auth.py:
   - hash_password/verify_password wrapping argon2.PasswordHasher (VerifyMismatchError -> False).
   - Route handlers under /api/auth: signup, login, logout, me.
   - SessionAuth[User, ServerSideSessionBackend] from litestar.security.session_auth: retrieve_user_handler loads User by session["user_id"]; ServerSideSessionConfig (max_age ~30 days, httponly, samesite=lax, secure driven by env flag); FileStore for session data (path from env, default .sessions/ next to DB); exclude /api/auth/signup + /api/auth/login + all non-/api paths (static + SPA fallback stay public).
4. Endpoint behavior:
   - POST /api/auth/signup {email, password, invite_code}: invite checked against DTASKS_INVITE_CODE env var via secrets.compare_digest; email lowercased/stripped, must contain "@"; password min 8 chars; duplicate email -> 409; success -> create user, seed_starter_data(session, user.id), request.set_session({"user_id": user.id}), 201 {id, email}.
   - POST /api/auth/login {email, password}: any failure (unknown email or bad password) -> 401 with identical generic detail "Invalid email or password"; verify against a dummy hash when user not found to equalize timing. Success sets session.
   - POST /api/auth/logout: request.clear_session() -> 204.
   - GET /api/auth/me: {id, email} from request.user.
   - Rate-limit /api/auth/* (litestar RateLimitConfig, e.g. 10/min) at the auth router level.
5. seed.py: seed_if_empty(session) becomes seed_starter_data(session, user_id) — drop the emptiness guard, stamp user_id on the 4 projects + 4 habits. Remove seeding from lifespan in main.py.
6. schemas.py: msgspec Structs SignupPayload, LoginPayload, UserOut.
7. conftest.py: set DTASKS_INVITE_CODE for tests via monkeypatch. The client fixture now signs up a default user (AsyncTestClient keeps the session cookie automatically), so all existing tests keep passing with per-user seeded data. Add a make_client(email) factory fixture for multi-user tests — each user needs its own AsyncTestClient because the cookie jar is per client.

## TDD plan — write tests/test_auth.py first, in this order
1. test_anonymous_api_request_gets_401 — GET /api/projects without session -> 401 (red against current code).
2. test_signup_creates_account_with_starter_data — signup -> 201 {id,email}; GET /api/auth/me -> 200; GET /api/projects -> 4 starter projects; GET /api/habits -> 4 habits.
3. test_signup_rejects_wrong_invite_code — 403; subsequent /me -> 401 (no session created).
4. test_signup_rejects_duplicate_email_case_insensitive — "Foo@x.com" then "foo@X.COM" -> 409.
5. test_signup_rejects_short_password — 7 chars -> 400.
6. test_login_logout_roundtrip — logout -> /me 401; login -> /me 200.
7. test_login_failures_are_indistinguishable — wrong password vs unknown email: same status and same body.
8. test_spa_routes_stay_public — GET / (non-/api path) is not 401.
Regression gate: existing test_projects.py stays green via the authed client fixture.
acceptance_criteria: Signup with correct invite code creates a user and starts a session; wrong invite code rejected; login/logout work; GET /api/auth/me returns user when authenticated, 401 otherwise; all /api/* except /api/auth/* and static files return 401 without a session; new user has starter demo data; alembic upgrade head builds the schema from scratch.
close_reason: Implemented on multitenancy branch, commit d76e2bc: auth endpoints, sessions, alembic, 13 tests green, real-server smoke test passed

title: Multitenancy: per-user auth and data isolation
description: Turn dtasks into a multi-user service for friends as beta-testers. Email/password accounts gated by an invite code (no OAuth), cookie sessions via Litestar SessionAuth, per-user data isolation enforced in every query (cross-tenant access returns 404), landing page with signup/login, starter demo data per new user, existing DB data migrated to the owner's account. Stack stays Litestar + SQLAlchemy + SQLite on a single VPS. Design agreed in chat 2026-07-03.
acceptance_criteria: Two users can sign up and use the app concurrently; neither can read or modify the other's projects/tasks/habits by any API call; unauthenticated requests to /api/* (except /api/auth/*) get 401; the app is deployed behind HTTPS with an invite code. (Data migration descoped: owner re-enters data manually.)

title: Allow changing order of projects in Personal and Work sections
description: Enable up/down controls on sidebar project list items and support reordering on the backend.

title: Allow assigning tasks to Today or Week from Review view
description: Add Today and Week toggle buttons to open tasks in the Review view, matching the styling and behavior of the Plan view.

title: Allow renaming existing projects from the UI
description: Backend PATCH /api/projects/{id} already accepts 'name' (ProjectPatch), but the frontend renders the project title as static text in ProjectView with no edit affordance. Users who create a project with the wrong name must delete it and re-create all tasks. Add click-to-rename on the project title in ProjectView, following the TaskRow click-to-edit idiom (Enter=save, Escape=cancel, blur=save, empty input falls back to old name). Add i18n keys in en+ru.
close_reason: Implemented click-to-rename on project title in ProjectView; verified end-to-end with Playwright against the running app

title: i18n phase 4: Cyrillic font subset + Russian visual pass
description: Plus Jakarta Sans ships no base Cyrillic (U+0400-045F), only cyrillic-ext; Russian falls back to system sans. Load a Cyrillic-only companion subset (e.g. @fontsource/inter cyrillic) after PJS in --font-sans so Latin stays pixel-identical. Then click through every view in Russian: 230px sidebar, btn-s buttons, tracked uppercase micro-labels (START HERE), text ~20-30% longer. Part of dtask-5lq.
close_reason: Inter cyrillic subset backs Plus Jakarta Sans for Russian glyphs (unicode-ranged, on-demand download verified in-browser); full ru visual pass over all 7 views incl. dark mode via screenshots - no overflow or fallback-font issues

title: i18n phase 3: Russian translations, plurals, localized dates
description: Translate ru.json (key set must mirror en.json - parity test enforces). Use i18next _one/_few/_many plural forms for counts. Replace hardcoded en-US locale in TodayView and HabitsView with a formatDate() helper in lib/dates.ts that uses the current language and uppercases the first letter of ru weekday/month names in headings. Part of dtask-5lq.
close_reason: ru.json fully translated (Must Have -> «Главное»), ru plurals for the must-limit alert, dates/weekday/month labels localized via Intl with capitalization helper; 51/51 vitest + real-browser ru check passed

title: i18n phase 2: extract all UI strings to the en catalog
description: Move ~110 hardcoded strings into en.json view by view: sidebar > today > plan > review > habits > project > welcome > shared components, including confirm()/alert() messages and ApiError fallbacks (map 401 login to a catalog key; generic localized fallback elsewhere). English only; zero visible behavior change. Part of dtask-5lq.
close_reason: All ~110 UI strings extracted to en.json (ru.json mirrors keys, translation is dtask-068); build/lint/vitest/e2e green with zero visible change

title: i18n phase 1: foundation — i18next setup, typed catalogs, language toggle
description: Install i18next + react-i18next. Add src/i18n/ (index.ts init, en.json, ru.json, typed resources via module augmentation). Add useLanguage() hook mirroring theme.ts (localStorage, document.documentElement.lang, changeLanguage) and an EN/RU toggle in the Sidebar footer. Init i18n with lng=en in src/test/setup.ts; add en/ru catalog key-parity test. Migrate the 4 sidebar nav labels as an end-to-end smoke test of the pipeline. Part of dtask-5lq.
close_reason: Implemented and verified: i18next foundation, typed catalogs, useLanguage + sidebar toggle, test setup; vitest 46/46, e2e signup flow + real-browser toggle check passed

title: i18n: support English and Russian UI languages
description: The app UI is English-only with ~110 hardcoded strings across 7 views and 5 components, plus en-US-hardcoded date formatting and browser confirm()/alert() messages. Add an i18n layer so the UI can render fully in English or Russian, with a language switcher, persisted preference, and correct Russian plural/date handling.
design: Library: react-i18next with statically bundled JSON catalogs (src/i18n/en.json, ru.json), typed keys via module augmentation. Language state: useLanguage() hook mirroring theme.ts (localStorage + document.documentElement.lang), EN/RU toggle in Sidebar footer. Dates: replace hardcoded 'en-US' locale in TodayView/HabitsView with current language; capitalize ru weekday in headings. Plurals: i18next _one/_few/_many/_other suffixes (Intl.PluralRules handles Russian). Backend stays English; frontend maps ApiError by status/known-case to localized messages (error codes on the wire as optional later step). FONT GOTCHA: Plus Jakarta Sans ships NO base Cyrillic subset (U+0400-045F) - only cyrillic-ext (historic letters); Russian would fall back to system sans. Fix: load a Cyrillic-only companion subset (e.g. Inter cyrillic) after PJS in --font-sans so Latin rendering stays identical. Lora (serif) already has Cyrillic. Tests: init i18n with lng=en in src/test/setup.ts so existing English assertions keep passing; add en/ru catalog key-parity test. Out of scope: seeded demo data, DTask brand name, backend content.
close_reason: All four phases implemented and verified; PR stack #23-#26 pending review/merge

title: Add habit creation (backend endpoint + UI form)
description:
Currently there is no way to add a new habit — the app only ships list_habits, set_habit_log, and delete_habit (backend/app/main.py:254-301); habits only exist via the per-user starter seed at signup (backend/app/seed.py). Once a user deletes a starter habit, there's no way to replace it, and users can't track any new habit beyond the 4 seeded ones.

## Implementation
1. Backend: POST /api/habits (name required, subtitle optional, default position = max+1 over the user's own habits, mirroring create_project's pattern at main.py:123-140). Add HabitCreate to schemas.py. Register the handler + reuse existing user-scoping conventions (Habit.user_id == user.id).
2. Frontend: api/client.ts createHabit(); api/hooks.ts useCreateHabit() (invalidate ['habits'] on success, mirroring useCreateProject); a small "New Habit" form/button in HabitsView.tsx, similar to AddTaskForm's pattern.

## Tests (TDD)
- Backend: test creating a habit returns 201 with the new habit (empty log); rejects blank name; new habit is scoped to the creating user (isolation test consistent with tests/test_isolation.py conventions).
- Frontend: hook test for useCreateHabit (POST + cache invalidation), component test for the new habit form in HabitsView.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/habits creates a habit for the authenticated user only and returns it; blank name rejected; UI has a visible way to add a new habit from the Habits view; existing habit tests (list/log/delete/isolation) remain green.
close_reason: Implemented POST /api/habits (backend, TDD) plus frontend createHabit/useCreateHabit and an AddHabitForm wired into HabitsView behind an Add toggle. All backend (pytest) and frontend (vitest) suites pass; verified end-to-end in a headless browser.

title: Delete habits (backend endpoint + frontend UI)
description:
Habits currently support list (GET /api/habits) and daily logging (PUT /api/habits/{id}/log), but there is no way to remove a habit once created. Add delete support end-to-end, following TDD (write the failing test first, then the minimal code to pass it).

## Backend (write tests first in backend/tests/test_habits.py, a new file)
1. test_delete_habit_removes_it_and_cascades_logs — mirrors test_delete_project_removes_it_and_cascades_tasks in test_projects.py. Seed/log a habit, DELETE /api/habits/{id}, assert 204, assert it's gone from GET /api/habits, and assert no orphan HabitLog rows remain (habit_logs.habit_id has ondelete=CASCADE already).
2. test_delete_missing_habit_returns_404 — DELETE /api/habits/999999 -> 404.
3. In test_isolation.py: test_cross_tenant_habit_delete_404 — user B cannot delete user A's habit (mirrors the existing test_cross_tenant_habit_404 pattern for the log endpoint) -> 404, and the habit still exists for user A afterward.

Then implement just enough to pass:
- Add a `_get_habit(session, habit_id, user_id) -> Habit` helper in backend/app/main.py, following the existing `_get_task`/`_get_project` pattern (raise NotFoundException if missing or not owned by user).
- Add `delete_habit` handler: `@delete("/api/habits/{habit_id:int}", status_code=204)`, delete + commit, same shape as delete_project/delete_task.
- Register `delete_habit` in `route_handlers`.

## Frontend (write test first)
1. In frontend/src/api/hooks.test.tsx, add a `describe('useDeleteHabit')` block mirroring the existing `useDeleteProject` tests (calls DELETE, invalidates/updates the ['habits'] query cache).
2. Add `deleteHabit: (id: number) => request<void>(\`/api/habits/${id}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })` to frontend/src/api/client.ts.
3. Add `useDeleteHabit()` to frontend/src/api/hooks.ts, mirroring `useDeleteTask`/`useDeleteProject`.
4. Wire a delete action into frontend/src/views/HabitsView.tsx's per-habit card (icon/button), using the same `confirm(...)` pattern as TaskRow.tsx before calling the mutation — no new modal component needed.

## Design decisions
- Reuse existing `_get_task`/`_get_project` helper pattern for `_get_habit` rather than inventing a new lookup style.
- Reuse the plain `window.confirm()` pattern from TaskRow.tsx for delete confirmation instead of building a custom dialog.
- Rely on the existing DB-level CASCADE on habit_logs.habit_id; no manual cleanup of logs needed in the handler.
acceptance_criteria:
- backend/tests/test_habits.py exists with test_delete_habit_removes_it_and_cascades_logs and test_delete_missing_habit_returns_404, both passing
- backend/tests/test_isolation.py has test_cross_tenant_habit_delete_404, passing
- DELETE /api/habits/{habit_id} implemented in backend/app/main.py, returns 204 on success, 404 when habit missing or owned by another user
- frontend/src/api/hooks.test.tsx has passing tests for useDeleteHabit
- frontend/src/api/client.ts and hooks.ts expose deleteHabit/useDeleteHabit
- HabitsView.tsx has a working delete action (with confirm) per habit, manually verified in the browser
- All backend (pytest) and frontend (vitest) test suites pass
close_reason: Implemented DELETE /api/habits/{id} + frontend delete action, TDD (backend and frontend tests written first and confirmed failing before implementation). All suites green.
